summary: Book's biggest risk stays distribution, not a single blow-up: 3 of 5 holdings carry live dilution/distribution overhangs (UUUU undrawn $300M ATM + escalating CEO Form 144 selling, NTSK post-lockup VC distribution, WRAP $200M baby-shelf) -- but re-check shows NONE drew a fresh offering this cycle (no new July 424B5 for any name), all held tickers reconcile to the broker, HDSN (fresh watchlist candidate) verified clean, and every scan candidate above/near the line is an already-vetted dilution/pump trap.

WRAP.note: $200M universal baby-shelf standing (last S-3 activity Feb 2026) on a sub-$100M post-pump name = drip-dilution capacity; re-check confirms NO July 424B5 draw. Only fresh items are CEO open-market BUYING (up to 209,353 sh) + positive distribution-rights deals -- capacity risk only, not active dilution. FinBERT -0.853 'unusual volume' item is noise. Position +$3.82 at $2.20.

UUUU.note: Active $300M ATM (2026-06-13) still undrawn per EDGAR/filings, but CEO Chalmers insider SELLING escalating: Form 144s for 200k (6/29), 100k/200k (7/2), +300k listed 7/9 via RBC -- distribution outweighs the smaller Bhappu/Hansen buys behind the score. Lorentzian k-NN -0.75 bearish. Position -$1.37 at ~$13.24.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution/shelf). Nightly audit flags broker_disagrees (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3%) but that is stale from 08:18 and converged intraday at last check; watch it -- bad price data would desync stops/P&L. FinBERT +0.861 positive. Position -$0.89.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$6.18 (~$14.28) on intact breakout, but post-IPO-lockup distribution persists: Form 144s 7/8 (1.75M Class A) + 7/9 atop Lightspeed conversions; ICONIQ's 610k open-market buy (7/10) only partly offsets the float unlock. No 424B5. Hiring pulse healthy (153 openings, no flag).

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback. Hiring pulse 24 openings, no flag. No fresh negative catalyst. Position +$0.56 at $5.45.

flags.why: Top non-held scan name (5.2, below line) with a 12-for-1 reverse split effective 7/14 (new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 compliance, on an unfunded 'AI+blockchain+crypto/stablecoin' pivot pump. Reverse-split-plus-crypto-pump, not a catalyst. FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline.

Scan candidate (5.17) that diluted ~30%: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement priced 6/25/closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.72 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's 7/7 buy came AFTER the raise.

Scan candidate (5.1) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.60, no fundamental driver. Reject on the pump veto; nightly audit also flags cache stale.

Scan candidate (below line) with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M sh out, active $300M S-3 shelf (7/8), 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved. On research avoid list.

Distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still appears on the scan.

On the daily watchlist but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, earliest close ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk; intel concurs do-not-chase.

Watchlist name that priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; ~$10 sits above mean analyst PT. Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On the research avoid list.

Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. Desk avoid list; not held. Nightly audit also flags a 2.7% source_mismatch (cache 3.825 vs yahoo 3.725).

Distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround. On research avoid list.

dilution_watch.note: $300M ATM usable now, re-check shows NO July draw. Insider selling escalating: Chalmers Form 144s via RBC -- 200k (6/29), 100k/200k (7/2), +300k listed 7/9.
$200M universal baby-shelf for a sub-$100M post-pump name; re-check confirms NO July 424B5 draw. Standing dilution capacity only; recent insider activity is BUYING.
$50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement; closed 6/26. 7/6 F-3 registers 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. No NEW July offering.
~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition; existing holders diluted to ~53% of combined co; DOJ second request pushes earliest close to ~9/29.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants; authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: Overtrading (carried): radar shows the book well over its 4-buy weekly cap. Surface as a standalone discipline flag independent of any single ticker.

UUUU: intel carries 'no boost' but still frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently; the CEO Chalmers Form 144 sale stack (200k 6/29, 100k/200k 7/2, 300k listed 7/9) outweighs those buys -- the insider signal behind the score is net-negative, reinforcing my elevated grade.
RIG: intel tags the Valaris deal 'do-not-chase' but still carries RIG on the active watchlist; I keep it an elevated DO-NOT-BUY given ~53% dilution and a binary vote/antitrust event.

HDSN: intel/research carry HDSN on the watchlist on Hartree/insider buying. I checked its capital structure -- effective but UNUSED $100M S-3 (12/16/2025) on a profitable name (Q1 rev $60.2M, +9%) with insiders and the 10%-owner BUYING; no active dilution, so I do NOT flag it. Noting only that the score should reflect a standing shelf exists.

AVO (Mission Produce): top non-held scan candidate on a +5.0 insider score, but that signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack takeover-style accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan -- not management conviction. No S-3/ATM (2026 dilution was the completed Calavo S-4/424B3, offset by a $100M buyback). Below the buy line so no flag, but the score's insider component is mis-attributed.
